🤖 Spending - AI Assistant (Ollama via Open WebUI)

Connect to Ollama LLM instances running on your network via Open WebUI

🔌 Connection Configuration

Configure connection to Ollama LLM via Open WebUI on TrueNAS or local network

URL where Open WebUI is accessible
Optional: API key for authentication
Name of the Ollama model to use

📊 Connection Status

Status: Unknown
Error:

Last Response Time: -

Model Info: -

💬 AI Interaction

Send prompts to the LLM and receive responses

Conversation History

📝 Implementation Notes

Integration Approaches:

  • Direct API: Communicate with Open WebUI's REST API endpoints for chat completions
  • WebSocket: Use WebSocket connection for streaming responses
  • Proxy: Set up a proxy endpoint on this server to handle Ollama/Open WebUI requests
  • Python Backend: Create a Python service using ollama-python library for more control

Current Implementation: UI mockup - backend integration pending. Configuration and prompts are stored locally for future implementation.

📸 Upload Account Screenshot

Upload a screenshot of your account dashboard to automatically update balances

ℹ️ How it works:
  • Upload a clear screenshot showing account names and balances
  • The system will extract account information using OCR
  • Existing accounts will be updated with new balances
  • New accounts found in the screenshot will be created automatically
  • Ambiguous account matches require your confirmation before import
  • Screenshots are deleted immediately after processing for security
Supported formats: JPG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 10MB
The date these account balances represent. Defaults to today. Cannot select future dates.

📱 Smart Mirror Dashboard

Configure the Smart Mirror dashboard accessible at /smart-mirror for display on home network devices:

🌅 Auto Theme Switching

Automatically switch between light and dark themes based on sunrise/sunset times. Light theme is active from 30 minutes before sunrise to 30 minutes after sunset. Configure separately for each orientation.

📱 Portrait Mode Auto Theme ▼
🖥️ Landscape Mode Auto Theme ▼

🎯 Interactive Grid Layout Editor

Editing Portrait Layout: Drag and drop widgets to reposition them. Use the resize handles on corners to change widget size. Only enabled widgets are shown. Changes are applied to the form below when you click "Apply Changes".

📦 Widget Status

Enabled Widget
Disabled Widget
Selected
Overlapping
Active / Inactive status badge
▶ 🔌 APIs and Connections

Configure API keys and connection settings used by multiple widgets. Managing credentials here reduces duplication and simplifies maintenance.

▶ 🌤️ OpenWeatherMap API

This API is used by the Weather Widget, Forecast Widget, Vacation Widget, and Smart Widget (Rain Forecast). Get a free API key at openweathermap.org/api.

This key will be used by all weather-related widgets (Weather, Forecast, Vacation, Rain Forecast)
Default location for weather widgets (can be overridden per widget)
Default units for all weather widgets
💡 Note:

Widget-specific settings (location, display options, refresh intervals) are configured in each widget's section below. Only the API credentials are centralized here for easier management.

▶ ✈️ AviationStack Flight API

This API is used for flight tracking in the Vacation Widget. It provides real-time flight status, delays, gate information, and more. Get a free API key at aviationstack.com (Free tier: 100 API calls/month).

Required for automatic flight tracking and validation
Enable to use real-time flight tracking
Free tier: 100 calls/month (adjust if you have a paid plan)
⚠️ Free Plan Limitations Detected

Your AviationStack Free Plan does not support real-time flight-specific queries (e.g. filtering by flight number or date). Flight number format validation is still available. For live departure/arrival times, delays, and gate info, consider upgrading your AviationStack plan.

📊 API Usage This Month
Loading... --%
⚠️ Warning: You're approaching your monthly API limit. Flight updates may be limited.
🔍 API Configuration Diagnostics
API Key Status: -
API Key Fingerprint: -
API Enabled: -
Provider: -
ℹ️ Troubleshooting: If validation fails, compare this fingerprint with the key you tested. They should match.
ℹ️ Rate Limiting & Update Schedule

To respect the free tier's 100 calls/month limit, flight data is automatically updated on this schedule:

  • Default: Once per day at 7am for all tracked flights
  • 3 days before flight: 3x daily (7am, noon, 5pm)
  • Final 6 hours: Hourly updates for real-time tracking

Flight data is cached between updates to minimize API calls. You can manually trigger an update from the tracked flights list below.

▶ 🚗 TomTom Traffic API

This API is used by the Drive-Time sub-widget in the Smart Widget to estimate travel times to upcoming calendar events with physical addresses. Get a free key at developer.tomtom.com (requires Routing API and Search API access).

Used by the Drive-Time sub-widget for geocoding and route calculations
💡 Note:

Geocoding results are cached for 24 hours; route calculations are refreshed every 30 minutes to balance accuracy and API quota. The home address starting point is configured in the Smart Widget → Drive-Time sub-widget settings below.

Widget Configuration

Enable and configure widgets for the Smart Mirror dashboard. Portrait mode uses a finer 4×6 grid (better vertical control), while landscape mode uses an 8×4 grid (better horizontal control).

▶ 🕐 Clock Widget

Add up to 3 additional cities/timezones to display. Remember to increase the widget height when adding timezones.

▶ 📅 Calendar Widget
Import events from Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar (webcal:// supported), or other iCal-compatible services. Enter one URL per line.
How long to cache calendar data before fetching fresh data (60-3600 seconds). Default: 600 seconds (10 minutes). This helps prevent rate limiting from calendar providers.
Cache Status:
▶ 🔍 Calendar Event Filters (Keyword-Based)
Filter or modify calendar events based on keywords in their title or description.
📋 How It Works
Create filter rules with keywords. When an event's title or description matches any keyword in a rule:
  • Hide: Event won't appear in the calendar display
  • Replace: Event title and/or description will be replaced with your custom text
Keywords are case-insensitive. Rules are applied to all connected calendars.
▶ 🌤️ Weather Widget (Current Conditions)
Leave blank to use default location from APIs & Connections section
Leave as default to use units from APIs & Connections
ℹ️ API Key: Configure your OpenWeatherMap API key in the APIs and Connections section above.
▶ 🌦️ Weather Forecast Widget
Leave blank to use default location from APIs & Connections section
Leave as default to use units from APIs & Connections
ℹ️ API Key: Configure your OpenWeatherMap API key in the APIs and Connections section above.
▶ 📰 News Widget
Enter RSS feed URLs from news sources you want to follow. One URL per line.
💡 Tips: Recommended News Sources & Regional Search
📡 Recommended RSS Feed Sources
  • Google News (regional) — automatically used for vacation & calendar-event headlines; no URL needed here.
  • BBC News — https://feeds.bbci.co.uk/news/rss.xml
  • Reuters — https://feeds.reuters.com/reuters/topNews
  • AP News — https://rsshub.app/apnews/topics/apf-topnews
  • NPR — https://feeds.npr.org/1001/rss.xml
  • Local outlets — search "[your city] news RSS" to find your local paper or TV station's feed.
📍 How Regional Search Works (Vacation & Calendar Headlines)

When displaying news for a vacation destination or calendar-event location, the system automatically optimises the Google News search query based on the address type:

  • Residential address (e.g. 123 Oak St, Austin, TX 78701) → search expanded to city + state (Austin, TX) for broader local coverage.
  • Landmark / venue (e.g. Fenway Park, Boston, MA) → landmark name used as the primary keyword, with city context for disambiguation.
  • City / region (e.g. Paris, TX or Paris, France) → used as-is; state or country context prevents mixing up similarly named cities.
✏️ Address & Destination Formatting Tips
  • For best results, always include the city and two-letter state (e.g. Springfield, IL) or city and country (e.g. Paris, France).
  • For venues and stadiums, use the official name followed by the city (e.g. Madison Square Garden, New York, NY).
  • Ambiguous city names (e.g. Paris, Springfield, Portland) should always include the state or country so the correct region is searched.
  • The Home Address set in the Smart Widget → Drive-Time section is used to detect when you are travelling to a different state, ensuring the search includes the destination's state name.
📍 Vacation Destination Headlines
Show headlines this many days before a vacation starts.
📅 Calendar Event Headlines
Show headlines this many days before a calendar event with a location.
🔢 Feed Display Order

Drag the feed types below (or use the ↑↓ buttons) to set the order in which they appear in the News widget. The top item is shown first.

▶ 🎵 Media Widget (Home Assistant)
ℹ️ Home Assistant connection (URL and token) is configured in the main Settings page under Home Assistant Integration.
Enter the entity IDs of your Home Assistant media players. Find these in Home Assistant under Developer Tools → States. The widget will display the first active (playing/paused) player, or the first available player if none are active.
▶ ✈️ Vacation Widget (Upcoming Vacations)

Configure vacation dates and destinations in the House → Vacation Dates section above. The widget will automatically display upcoming vacations with weather, timezone, and countdown information.

ℹ️ API Key: Configure your OpenWeatherMap API key in the APIs and Connections section above. The same key is used for vacation destination weather.
▶ 🌬️ Air Quality Widget (Compact AQI Display)
When enabled, the widget will glow green when air quality is "Good" (AQI=1) and temperature is 75°F or less, indicating favorable outdoor conditions.

The Air Quality Widget displays current AQI (Air Quality Index) and forecasts for today and tomorrow. It uses the OpenWeatherMap Air Pollution API to provide real-time air quality data including PM2.5, PM10, and other pollutants.

AQI Scale:
1 = Good (😊) | 2 = Fair (🙂) | 3 = Moderate (😐) | 4 = Poor (😷) | 5 = Very Poor (🤢)

ℹ️ API Key: Configure your OpenWeatherMap API key in the APIs and Connections section above. The same key is used for weather and air quality data.
▶ 📱 Smart Widget (Intelligent Multi-Widget Display)
▶ ⚙️ Display Settings
Choose how sub-widgets are displayed
Time between cycles in cycle mode
Maximum number of sub-widgets to show at once in simultaneous mode
In adaptive mode, widgets up to this size are stacked vertically; larger widgets cycle
Adaptive Mode: Small/medium widgets (rain, vacation, media) display as horizontally long tabs stacked vertically. Large widgets (party with extensive content) cycle separately.
▶ 🧩 Sub Widgets
▶ 🌩️ Extreme Weather
▶ 🌧️ Rain Expected
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode

Shows upcoming rain in the forecast (next 3–5 days). Requires Weather API configuration above.

▶ 🔥 High Heat
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode
Show sub-widget if any of the next 3 days exceeds this high (°F/°C)

Shows a High Heat alert if the forecast high temperature exceeds the configured maximum for any of the next 3 days.

▶ 🌡️ Temp Change
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode
Show sub-widget if day-to-day high changes by at least this amount

Shows a temperature change alert if the daily high shifts by more than the configured degrees between any two consecutive days in the next 3-day forecast.

ℹ️ Extreme Weather API: Configure your OpenWeatherMap API key and location in the APIs and Connections section above.
▶ ✈️ Vacation
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode

Displays the next upcoming vacation from House → Vacation page.

▶ 🎉 Party
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode

Shows upcoming party information from Party > Scheduling tab. Only displays when party date is set. On party day, this widget will display exclusively without cycling.

▶ 🎵 Home Assistant Media
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode
Hide widget after media has been paused for this many minutes. Set to 0 to never hide.

Shows when media is playing through Home Assistant. Requires Home Assistant configuration in the main Settings page.

One or more entity IDs, separated by commas
▶ 🔋 Battery & Charging
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode
How often to fetch updated data from Home Assistant (min 10 s)

Monitors battery and charging state of devices via Home Assistant. Uses the Home Assistant connection configured in the main Settings page. Device names are automatically kept in sync with Home Assistant — use the Override Name field on each device to set a custom display name.

Device Search & Discovery
Keyword to filter battery-related entities
Tracked Device Groups

No devices tracked yet. Use the search above to find and add devices.

📊 Live Status from Home Assistant
ℹ️ Each entry is a device group: it combines a battery-level entity with an optional charging-state sensor under one display name. The widget only shows when a device matches an active scenario (charging, fully charged, or low battery). Device names are automatically synced from Home Assistant unless an Override Name is set.
▶ 🚗 Drive-Time
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode

Scans calendar events for the next 2 days, finds those with physical addresses, and shows estimated drive time and live traffic delays using the TomTom API. Up to 5 events with addresses are processed per refresh; up to 3 are shown at once on the display.

Home Address
Full street address used as the origin for all drive-time calculations.
ℹ️ TomTom API Key: Configure your TomTom API key in the APIs and Connections section above. The same key is used for geocoding and route calculations.
▶ 🚀 SpaceX Launch (Vandenberg)
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode
Show widget when launch is within this many days (1–30)
Start flashing when launch is within this many hours (1–168)

Shows upcoming SpaceX launches from Vandenberg Space Force Base using the free Launch Library 2 API (no key required). Highlights launches near sunset (🌅 Sunset Launch! badge) for spectacular plume visibility. Data is refreshed every 30 minutes.

▶ 📷 QR Codes
Lower numbers = higher priority
How long to display in cycle mode

Generates up to two WiFi QR codes and two link QR codes directly on the Smart Mirror display. Only fully populated entries are shown to viewers.

ℹ️ About Smart Widget: The Smart Widget intelligently displays sub-widgets based on their content and relevance. It only shows sub-widgets when they have active content (e.g., rain in forecast, high heat alert, significant temp change, upcoming vacation, media playing, battery/charging alerts). Configure priority to control which sub-widgets appear first.

💾 Backup & Restore

Export all your site configurations and data to a backup file, or restore from a previously exported backup.

▶ 📤 Export Data
⚠️ Security Note:

For security, sensitive credentials (API keys, tokens, passwords) are NOT included in exports. You will need to re-enter these after importing.

The export will include:

  • Magic Mirror configuration (widget settings, layout)
  • Server settings and preferences
  • Vidiots configuration (excluding GitHub token)
  • Espresso configuration and data
  • Finance data (accounts, demographics, settings)
  • Useful links
  • Connected devices
  • Drink mixer recipes and ingredients
  • Tournament data
  • Client configurations
▶ 📥 Import Data
⚠️ Warning:

Importing a backup will overwrite existing configurations. Existing API keys and passwords will be preserved if they are not included in the import file.

Select a .json backup file previously exported from this system

📋 Backup Preview

🌐 SFTP Backup Management

Configure SFTP connection to automatically upload and download backup files to/from remote storage (e.g., Synology NAS, remote server).

▶ ⚙️ SFTP Configuration
🔐 Security:

All credentials are encrypted using AES-256-GCM and stored securely. Credentials are never exposed to the frontend.

Directory on server to store backups (must exist)
Leave blank to keep existing password
Paste your private key in OpenSSH format. Leave blank to keep existing key. Only needed if your private key is encrypted with a passphrase
▶ 🔍 Connection Test & Diagnostics

Test your SFTP connection to verify settings before uploading backups.

▶ 📤 Upload Backup to SFTP

Generate a new backup and upload it directly to your SFTP server.

Preparing backup...

Browse and download backup files from your SFTP server.

API Endpoints:

  • GET /api/status - Server status for external tools
  • POST /api/webhook - Receive webhooks from external services
  • GET /api/data - Data endpoint for integrations

Integration URLs:

  • Home Assistant: Use http://your-server:3000/api/status as a RESTful sensor
  • Cockpit: Monitor via http://your-server:3000/api/status

  • Historical balances will be restored from the balance history if available
  • Transaction history will be split back to the original accounts
  • If balance history is incomplete, accounts will be created with $0 balance (you can update manually)
  • This operation creates an audit trail but cannot be automatically reversed

When enabled, the destination's local time will appear in the clock widget 1 week before, during, and 5 days after the vacation (max 3 clocks total).

✈️ Flight Information

ℹ️ Note: Add flight details here. Admin can validate flights before enabling tracking. Configure the AviationStack API key in Smart Mirror → APIs and Connections to enable real-time validation. Free Plan users will see ⚠️ Format validated — the flight number format is checked but live data is not available. Upgrade your AviationStack plan for full real-time tracking.
